| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5793503f-aa0c-4b7e-9d3c-3ba367d633a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I'm going to ask you to make a critical donation to help us close a real gap in our budget -- but first, let me explain:
Our campaign runs digital ads constantly. It's one of the most effective ways we can compete. Those ads put our message directly in front of persuadable voters in every corner of our district - voters we might not reach otherwise.
The problem? _A single week of digital ads in a competitive House district can cost $10,000 or more._
**And national Republicans can afford a hell of a lot more ads than we can. They have $300 MILLION sitting in the bank, ready to spend in districts exactly like ours -- flooding voters' feeds with misinformation while we scramble to keep up.**
So I made a call. I met with my team and greenlit a major new digital ad push... _even though the money wasn 't in the budget._
#### **[But now, we need to come up with $15,000 to close the gap that decision created, and I need your help to do it. Can I count on you to chip in a few dollars before 11:59 p.m.?](https://substack.com/redirect/5793503f-aa0c-4b7e-9d3c-3ba367d633a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ CHIP IN TODAY](https://substack.com/redirect/5793503f-aa0c-4b7e-9d3c-3ba367d633a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The GOP isn't slowing down. They're spreading misinformation 24/7, and our ads are how we correct the record and lay out what's actually at stake for voters in Maryland's 5th District.
But if we don't close this funding gap, we may have to pull those ads entirely. I don't want to go dark. Not now. Not when we're this close.
#### **[So please, rush whatever you can afford ASAP to help us fight back, win this race, and flip the House blue:](https://substack.com/redirect/5793503f-aa0c-4b7e-9d3c-3ba367d633a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ CHIP IN TODAY](https://substack.com/redirect/5793503f-aa0c-4b7e-9d3c-3ba367d633a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Let's do this,
Harry

Hello,
2026 is our best chance ever to defeat Lauren Boebert. But right now, we have a problem – and I need your help.
**Our next fundraising deadline is at midnight tonight, but we’re still falling short of our critical $25,000 goal and what we need to send Lauren Boebert packing.** [**So please, will you make a monthly donation right now to help us get back on track to defeat Lauren Boebert and take back the House for Democrats this November?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129338879/614004699/156431970?refcode=20260412pm-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOWE3YWExYzctY2IzNi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=r-ZAb3fOWtI05MPZ0ZWcjic8JuAoggfOqmT6JpEr50Y=)
As you read this, Lauren Boebert is raising money from corporate PACs and mega-donors like Elon Musk. And her Super PAC allies are gearing up to spend millions against us.
The Republicans are terrified because they have the slimmest of majorities, and if we defeat Lauren Boebert and flip this seat blue, Democrats are virtually guaranteed to retake control of the House.
But here’s the deal: I’m not taking money from corporate PACs. I don’t have Super PACs ready to pour millions into this race. **Instead, I’m counting entirely on grassroots donors like you if we’re going to finally put an end to Lauren Boebert’s embarrassing political career.**
[**So please, will you make a monthly donation to our campaign right now – every dollar you can afford – to help me defeat Lauren Boebert, flip this seat blue, and take back the House for Democrats?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129338880/614004751/156431970?refcode=20260412pm-nd&recurring=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTgxNzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOWE3YWExYzctY2IzNi1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=r-ZAb3fOWtI05MPZ0ZWcjic8JuAoggfOqmT6JpEr50Y=)

name: Voter Action Project Team
subject: The suppressive changes from these state laws
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
These state-level bills -- based on the suppressive SAVE America Act, which is blocked in the Senate -- will harm voter turnout and block eligible voters from casting valid ballots. Our efforts to protect the fundamental right to vote depend on stopping them, one state at a time.
Fuel our work to defend voting rights with $35 or whatever you can right now >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=041226_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project[https://voteractionproject.org/]
Democracy Docket: As SAVE America Act stalls, GOP states are quietly enacting their own proof of citizenship laws [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=041226_3&amount=35]
When the House rammed the SAVE America Act (the new version of the SAVE Act) to passage earlier this year, we turned to the Senate to stop it. So far that effort has been successful, but that doesn't mean the threat is over.
State by state, opponents of voting rights in state legislatures around the country are pushing their own version of this suppressive legislation ahead of the midterm elections , folks.
Ten states have laws -- four of which passed during this second Trump administration -- that require proof of citizenship when registering to vote. Florida, Mississippi, South Dakota and Utah all enacted proof of citizenship voting bills in the past two weeks . In as many as 12 other states, bills are moving through state legislatures. If they all pass, that would mean nearly half the states in our country have passed mini versions of the SAVE America Act .
Defenders of voting rights know that non-citizen voting is already illegal, extremely rare, and that these bills are written in ways that could disenfranchise millions of eligible women across the country. That hasn't stopped anti-voting groups from pressuring states to pass these new restrictive voting laws, because the suppression is their goal.
Please, Team, we need to stop this dangerous legislation wherever we can. Pitch in with $35 or whatever you can and ensure millions of women's voices continue to be heard in our elections >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=041226_3&amount=35]

Opponents of voting rights in Congress, state legislatures, governor's mansions, and in the White House favor bills like the SAVE America Act because they know it could suppress millions of eligible voters, changing the composition of the electorate and potentially empowering them and their party for years to come.
These bills are all being fast-tracked by vote suppressors to be in place before November, folks. This movement is a stunning assault on our fundamental right to vote, and we can not afford to let this kind of anti-democratic legislation become law.
Catherine Engelbrecht, the founder of suppressive organization True the Vote, described the right-wing conspiracy theory group’s focus during a recent livestream: “ If the SAVE America Act isn’t going to pass then I’m going to use every tool at my disposal to try and get states to react to something ,” Engelbrecht said.
These dark-money suppression groups are willing to go state-by-state to enact their agenda and we have no choice but to match them with our grassroots power.
The consequences of these bills could be catastrophic for our democracy. Chip in with $35 or whatever you can today to stop more of these voter suppression bills from becoming law >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=041226_3&amount=35]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=041226_3&amount=20]

clean_body: These state-level bills -- based on the suppressive SAVE America Act, which is blocked in the Senate -- will harm voter turnout and block eligible voters from casting valid ballots. Our efforts to protect the fundamental right to vote depend on stopping them, one state at a time. Fuel our work to defend voting rights with $35 or whatever you can right now >> [] Voter Action Project[] Democracy Docket: As SAVE America Act stalls, GOP states are quietly enacting their own proof of citizenship laws [] When the House rammed the SAVE America Act (the new version of the SAVE Act) to passage earlier this year, we turned to the Senate to stop it. So far that effort has been successful, but that doesn't mean the threat is over. State by state, opponents of voting rights in state legislatures around the country are pushing their own version of this suppressive legislation ahead of the midterm elections , folks. Ten states have laws -- four of which passed during this second Trump administration -- that require proof of citizenship when registering to vote. Florida, Mississippi, South Dakota and Utah all enacted proof of citizenship voting bills in the past two weeks . In as many as 12 other states, bills are moving through state legislatures. If they all pass, that would mean nearly half the states in our country have passed mini versions of the SAVE America Act . Defenders of voting rights know that non-citizen voting is already illegal, extremely rare, and that these bills are written in ways that could disenfranchise millions of eligible women across the country. That hasn't stopped anti-voting groups from pressuring states to pass these new restrictive voting laws, because the suppression is their goal. Please, Team, we need to stop this dangerous legislation wherever we can. name: Nancy Lacore
subject: Hegseth dismissed me, but I’m not done serving
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
Hi there,
After serving our country for 35 years in the Navy, I was removed from my position by Pete Hegseth without reason.
I spent my entire life in service to this country, always putting the mission and the people above myself. But when I was forced out, I had a choice: go out quietly, or find a way to keep serving.
I’ve always lived by three simple ideals: honor, integrity, and respect. That’s why I’m running for Congress in South Carolina’s first district. If you believe that’s what we need in Washington right now, please consider splitting $20 between my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR TO MY CAMPAIGN TODAY! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
The day I was removed from my position, I was forced to leave behind my life’s work. I never went back to the Pentagon or wore the uniform again.
But this story isn’t just about me. Throughout the first year of Donald Trump’s second term, every woman serving as a three or four-star admiral in the Navy has been fired, forced out, or reassigned. This wasn’t an isolated incident. It’s a pattern.
When leadership decisions are driven by politics instead of integrity, our families, communities, and nation pay the price.
That’s why I’m running for Congress: to keep serving, to stand up when it’s hardest, and to fight for leadership that puts country over politics every time.
Early support in races like this matters most. We have a long road ahead of us if we’re going to fill this open seat and make the government work for the people again. If you can, please split $20 between my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild to help me hit the ground running and prove that service to our country shouldn’t end just because someone in power says it should. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
CHIP IN TO DEFEAT MAGA AND FLIP THIS SEAT! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-lacore]
With my deepest respect and gratitude,
Nancy Lacore
Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide.

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: “not possible”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em?amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sjj-em?amount=25]
According to Donald Trump, it's "not possible" for the federal government to fund Medicare, Medicaid, or child care because "we're fighting wars."
How convenient.
Trump is choosing to drive our country deeper into debt for a war Americans from all political persuasions overwhelmingly oppose, all while telling you we can't afford basic needs like health care and child care.
We are the richest country in the world, and if we’re able to spend more than $1 billion a day on another reckless war of choice in the Middle East, then refusing to fund programs like Medicaid was never about a budget problem.
If we truly had a budget problem, this administration wouldn’t be proposing increasing the Pentagon budget to $1.5 trillion next year — yes, that’s trillion with a T.
This is a policy choice.
Now Republicans in Congress are considering further cuts to Americans’ health care to pay for this war. So just to be clear: there's apparently unlimited money for Donald Trump's expensive, illegal, and reckless war in Iran. But Medicare? Medicaid? Universal child care that lets parents actually go to work? Sorry, that’s "not possible."
I call bullsh*t.
Americans need good health care and child care that they can afford, not more bombs being dropped in our names using our tax dollars. If you agree, please add your name to tell Congress you want health care and child care, not more war. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/qAnZp7QitEiElH0ycObo3g2]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://secure.ngpvan.com/qAnZp7QitEiElH0ycObo3g2]
We don’t have to accept this trade-off.
–Sara

Congresswoman Sara Jacobs
California’s 51st District

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 4/12/2026
body:
What really happened at the GOP assembly in Pueblo this weekend
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**April 12, 2026** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST**
| | Dear Rocky Mountain Voice family,
This weekend, Colorado Republicans came to Pueblo to choose candidates.
They left having done that—and still debating who should have a say in choosing them.
What unfolded over the course of a long delayed and at times [contentious assembly](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) produced clear outcomes in major races while also setting in motion a legal step that could reshape how those candidates are selected in the future. Let's dig in.
**[The official outcomes?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** In the governor’s race, Scott Bottoms and Victor Marx both advanced (with very different visions for Colorado), while Mark Baisley was the only Senate candidate to make the ballot. Michael Allen will face late entry David Willson for AG, James Wiley advanced solo for secretary of state, and Kevin Grantham secured the treasurer spot with strong delegate support.
And across the board, you saw candidates leaning hard into the issues we’ve been covering all year—crime, elections, cost of living, the direction of the state.
So yes—the candidates are set. But that’s not what people are still talking about.
**The real tension came when the conversation shifted to the primary system.**
Right now in Colorado, unaffiliated voters can vote in either party’s primary. That’s been the rule for years. But this week, delegates voted to support a legal effort to change that—to limit Republican ballots to Republicans only.
And that’s where things got... complicated. Because depending on who you ask, this is either _protecting the party or shrinking it._
Some people see it as common sense—Republicans should pick Republican candidates. Others see it as a risk—especially in a state where [unaffiliated voters make up a huge chunk of the electorate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
And you could feel that divide in the room. It didn’t help that the day itself started a little messy. There were issues with credentialing, delays getting started, even questions about how votes were being counted.
Nothing that changed the outcome—but enough to add to the frustration.
And then later, more back-and-forth over rules, resolutions, and whether certain proposals should even be allowed on the floor.
If you zoom out, it all points to the same thing: [A party trying to figure out not just who it is—but how it operates moving forward.](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
This isn’t just about a single vote at a convention. It’s about where the Republican Party in Colorado goes from here.
Do you open the tent wider? Or do you double down on the base? Do you prioritize turnout? Or do you prioritize control?
Those aren’t easy questions. And they’re not getting answered overnight. But one thing is clear after this week: That debate is no longer happening behind closed doors. It’s happening out in the open—and it’s not going away anytime soon.
**Read our full recap from the GOP assembly here:** ➡️ [GOP advances candidates as convention backs effort to limit primary ballots to Republicans](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
In truth & liberty, Team RMV
---
| | |
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[While Colorado cuts care for its most vulnerable, it continues funding undocumented children in a $96 million program](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) “This is selfish.” That’s how Jon Caldara opened his April 1 column, writing not about politics, but about his son. He leads Denver’s Independence Institute and has long been a free-market voice in Colorado.
**[Colorado fought scrutiny—until a lawsuit forced a cleanup](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY RMV EDITORIAL BOARD, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Back in 2019, Colorado’s voter rolls were already showing the problem—if anyone in charge had been willing to look at them. Forty counties had more registered voters than eligible citizens. Call it whatever you want—but it’s not normal._
**[El Paso Co. found a way to cut undeliverable ballots and clean voter rolls—Colorado leaders looked away](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY BOB COOPER, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _This story should get your attention and maybe even make you angry if you care about election integrity issues—or simply about wasting taxpayer dollars in every county in Colorado. Get this: El Paso County has been implementing a common-sense voter roll maintenance process, a true “Gold Standard” process, to reduce election costs and clean up the “dirty,” bloated voter rolls._

|
---
**LEGISLATIVE SESSION**
_**Quick Summary of the Week:**_ 🏛️ _**In the Senate:** Senate Republicans highlighted concerns this week over _[HB26-1126](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_ , a bill they say could put small gun shops at risk by adding new regulations and steep fines for noncompliance, as it moves through the legislative process. At the same time, lawmakers celebrated a bipartisan win with the signing of _[HB26-1031](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_ to protect Colorado agriculture labeling standards, while also advancing public safety efforts like _[SB26-072](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_ , which increases penalties for vehicular homicide and assault in response to rising roadway fatalities._
🏛️ _**In the House:** House lawmakers spent the week deep in budget debates, working through a projected $1.5 billion shortfall while Republicans pushed amendments aimed at cutting bureaucracy and refocusing spending on core priorities like public safety, veterans, and vulnerable populations. Several proposals also moved forward or are scheduled for hearings, including measures on age verification for online content, military protection orders, and changes to homicide statutes, while Republicans raised concerns about bills impacting property taxes, immigration enforcement cooperation, and legal liability for therapists following a recent Supreme Court ruling._
|
---
**[As Drought Deepens, Colorado Still Has No Rules For Data Center Water Use](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY SHAINA COLE, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) In Aurora, data center proposals run through a simple filter. City officials compare total water use against how much of that water won’t come back—lost to evaporation. If either number gets too high, the project doesn’t move forward. When a developer wants to build in Denver, there is no matrix.
**[Colorado lawmakers move to sidestep Supreme Court ruling on therapy speech](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[SEY EVERYTHING](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The U.S. Supreme Court ruled on March 31, 2026, in Chiles v. Salazar (8-1 decision, with Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson dissenting) that Colorado’s 2019 ban on “conversion therapy” for minors violates the First Amendment’s free-speech protections as applied to talk therapy._
__
**[Colorado budget showdown: GOP forces full reading of 661-page bill, halting debate](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A procedural battle at the Colorado Capitol boiled over on Wednesday night, when a House Republican demanded the 661‐page state budget be read aloud in full — a move that delayed the advance of the proposed $46.8 billion spending plan.

**[READ MORE ON LEGISLATIVE SESSION](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**STATE**
**[She moved her company to Colorado: Seven months later she decided to leave](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Heather Florio didn’t move her company to Colorado for a short stay. When she arrived in early 2025, she thought she was putting down roots. “We came here... at the beginning of January, 2025 with anticipation of this being our permanent home.” About seven months in, she said the company was having to leave.
**[From Kazakhstan to Colorado: Secretary of State candidate James Wiley’s plan to scrap electronic voting machines](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) James Wiley was 7 on Election Night 2000. He remembers the decorations from his birthday staying up for two more weeks while the country waited to find out who had won. He didn’t understand hanging chads or voting machines.

**[A system under scrutiny: Colorado’s election system faces clash over how it’s verified](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to compile a nationwide citizenship list and share it with state election officials, it set off a debate in Colorado that hasn’t slowed.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[DHS Chief Floats Ending International Flights To Sanctuary Cities](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [TOWNHALL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin unveiled a new proposal on Monday night to eliminate Customs and Border Patrol at international airports in sanctuary cities, effectively ending all international travel from the location.
**[Colorado Faces Backlash Over Law Limiting Attorney Cooperation With Federal Authorities](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Colorado’s tourism slogan, “It’s Our Nature,” has a menacing meaning for free speech advocates. Colorado is now arguably the most anti-free speech state in the union, pushing an array of measures attacking those with opposing social and political views._
__
**[From imminent threat to no threat: Why the Iran narrative suddenly changed](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[AMERICAN THINKER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Not long ago, Iran was described as an imminent threat. Now we are told it wasn’t a threat at all. What changed? Not the facts. The politics. That shift is playing out in real time as the narrative around the Iran war evolves.
_**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[When a remodel turns into a $60,000 lesson: One builder’s run-in with Colorado’s regulatory system](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When Rob Treta started expanding a small home for his girlfriend in Arvada, he thought he was dealing with a familiar problem—delays. “I submitted the plans and I said, ‘Hey, tell me what I need to do,’” Treta said. “They told me three to four weeks. It took 22 weeks. Nobody ever mentioned asbestos.” __
**[Suburban Voters In Lakewood Reject Multi Unit Housing Expansion Plan](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Lakewood voters rebuffed zoning changes approved by the City Council that seek to allow for more housing “density,” according to the initial and unofficial count Tuesday night.
__
**[Ambitious Climate Targets In Boulder Clash With Energy Realities](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[JUST THE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When it comes to anti-fossil fuel policies, few cities have pursued them with as much gusto as Boulder, Colorado. In 2006, Boulder became one of the first local governments in the nation to adopt emission reduction targets.

**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Follow the money faster: New tool unlocks Colorado spending data in minutes](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO ACCOUNTABILITY PROJECT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _I have mentioned (and used) the TOPS system, our state’s online checkbook register, multiple times. It’s a great way to see who our state is paying and for what. I noticed recently (within the last 6 months) that the people who run it made it significantly harder to use: I’m not sure why, but at some point they made it so you can only go month by month.**_ __
**[Colorado bills spark concern over parental rights and religious freedom](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CHRISTIAN HOME EDUCATORS OF COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Colorado is becoming much more antagonistic towards families and people of faith. Our leadership appears to place more importance on ideology, secrecy, and crime when introducing and debating bills. The last few weeks have been an example of that mindset._
__
**[Colorado Supreme Court sides with transparency in child abuse hotline case](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO ACCOUNTABILITY PROJECT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The CFOIC article linked at bottom details a recent ruling by the CO Supreme Court. Five years on from when the suit was first filed, the state’s highest court ruled that the Colorado Sun and 9News have a right to some records from the state’s child abuse hotline pertaining to group homes for children._
__
**_[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** Email [submit@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com).
|
---
**💵** **DID YOU KNOW?** In **Colorado's first attempt at statehood** in 1864, proponents tried to keep costs low and set salaries at $1,000 for Secretary of State and $400 for Attorney General.
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "The family, rather than the individual, has always been and remains today the basic building block of our society, though its hold has clearly been weakening—one of the most unfortunate consequences of the growth of government paternalism." – Milton Friedman
|

Get this: El Paso County has been implementing a common-sense voter roll maintenance process, a true “Gold Standard” process, to reduce election costs and clean up the “dirty,” bloated voter rolls. | LEGISLATIVE SESSION Quick Summary of the Week: 🏛️ In the Senate: Senate Republicans highlighted concerns this week over , a bill they say could put small gun shops at risk by adding new regulations and steep fines for noncompliance, as it moves through the legislative process. At the same time, lawmakers celebrated a bipartisan win with the signing of to protect Colorado agriculture labeling standards, while also advancing public safety efforts like , which increases penalties for vehicular homicide and assault in response to rising roadway fatalities. 🏛️ In the House: House lawmakers spent the week deep in budget debates, working through a projected $1.5 billion shortfall while Republicans pushed amendments aimed at cutting bureaucracy and refocusing spending on core priorities like public safety, veterans, and vulnerable populations. Several proposals also moved forward or are scheduled for hearings, including measures on age verification for online content, military protection orders, and changes to homicide statutes, while Republicans raised concerns about bills impacting property taxes, immigration enforcement cooperation, and legal liability for therapists following a recent Supreme Court ruling. | BY SHAINA COLE, In Aurora, data center proposals run through a simple filter. City officials compare total water use against how much of that water won’t come back—lost to evaporation. If either number gets too high, the project doesn’t move forward. When a developer wants to build in Denver, there is no matrix. The U.S. Supreme Court ruled on March 31, 2026, in Chiles v. Salazar (8-1 decision, with Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson dissenting) that Colorado’s 2019 ban on “conversion therapy” for minors violates the First Amendment’s free-speech protections as applied to talk therapy. A procedural battle at the Colorado Capitol boiled over on Wednesday night, when a House Republican demanded the 661‐page state budget be read aloud in full — a move that delayed the advance of the proposed $46.8 billion spending plan. | STATE BY JEN SCHUMANN, Heather Florio didn’t move her company to Colorado for a short stay. When she arrived in early 2025, she thought she was putting down roots. “We came here... at the beginning of January, 2025 with anticipation of this being our permanent home.” About seven months in, she said the company was having to leave. BY JEN SCHUMANN, James Wiley was 7 on Election Night 2000. He remembers the decorations from his birthday staying up for two more weeks while the country waited to find out who had won. He didn’t understand hanging chads or voting machines. BY JEN SCHUMANN, When President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to compile a nationwide citizenship list and share it with state election officials, it set off a debate in Colorado that hasn’t slowed. | NATIONAL Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin unveiled a new proposal on Monday night to eliminate Customs and Border Patrol at international airports in sanctuary cities, effectively ending all international travel from the location. Colorado’s tourism slogan, “It’s Our Nature,” has a menacing meaning for free speech advocates. Colorado is now arguably the most anti-free speech state in the union, pushing an array of measures attacking those with opposing social and political views. Not long ago, Iran was described as an imminent threat. Now we are told it wasn’t a threat at all. What changed? Not the facts. The politics. That shift is playing out in real time as the narrative around the Iran war evolves. | LOCAL BY JEN SCHUMANN, When Rob Treta started expanding a small home for his girlfriend in Arvada, he thought he was dealing with a familiar problem—delays. “I submitted the plans and I said, ‘Hey, tell me what I need to do,’” Treta said. “They told me three to four weeks. It took 22 weeks. Nobody ever mentioned asbestos.” Lakewood voters rebuffed zoning changes approved by the City Council that seek to allow for more housing “density,” according to the initial and unofficial count Tuesday night. When it comes to anti-fossil fuel policies, few cities have pursued them with as much gusto as Boulder, Colorado. In 2006, Boulder became one of the first local governments in the nation to adopt emission reduction targets. | COMMENTARY I have mentioned (and used) the TOPS system, our state’s online checkbook register, multiple times. It’s a great way to see who our state is paying and for what. I noticed recently (within the last 6 months) that the people who run it made it significantly harder to use: I’m not sure why, but at some point they made it so you can only go month by month. Colorado is becoming much more antagonistic towards families and people of faith. Our leadership appears to place more importance on ideology, secrecy, and crime when introducing and debating bills. The last few weeks have been an example of that mindset. The CFOIC article linked at bottom details a recent ruling by the CO Supreme Court. Five years on from when the suit was first filed, the state’s highest court ruled that the Colorado Sun and 9News have a right to some records from the state’s child abuse hotline pertaining to group homes for children. _ | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? Email mailto:submit@rockymountainvoice.com. | 💵 DID YOU KNOW? In Colorado's first attempt at statehood in 1864, proponents tried to keep costs low and set salaries at $1,000 for Secretary of State and $400 for Attorney General. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | QUOTE OF THE DAY** "The family, rather than the individual, has always been and remains today the basic building block of our society, though its hold has clearly been weakening—one of the most unfortunate consequences of the growth of government paternalism." – Milton Friedman | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. With optimism and exctement building for the 2026 elections, this debate brings together leading Republican candidates to share their ideas and vision for Oregon’s future. Attend in Person A limited number of tickets are available for $25 through Wednesday at 5 PM. No tickets will be sold at the door. 👉 Get In-Person Tickets ($25) Watch Online (Free) If you prefer to join from home, you can register to watch the livestream at no cost. 👉 Register to Watch Live (Free) "" Event Details Date: Thursday, April 16 Time: 7:00 – 8:30 PM Location: NW Events, Hillsboro This event will cover key issues impacting Oregonians, including housing, jobs and business, natural resources, and public safety—giving voters a chance to hear directly from candidates in one place. Whether you attend in person or online, we hope you’ll take advantage of this opportunity to stay informed and engaged. name: Save our Postal Service (via VPP)
subject: “USPS will run out of money next year” >>
body:
This could be the end.
| **__BREAKING:__**
**Postmaster General Warns Postal Service “Will Run Out of Money in 2027,” Could Halt All Deliveries** [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=6&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI) | [**WE NEED A SURGE OF SUPPORT TO SAVE USPS.
Do you APPROVE of your local Post Office?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=7&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=8&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI) | [I APPROVE OF MY LOCAL POST OFFICE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=9&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI)
---
[I DON’T APPROVE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=10&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI)
**__THIS IS TERRIFYING NEWS:__**
Destroying the Postal Service has been Trump’s plan ALL ALONG. And now, he’s even closer to eliminating it than we thought possible...

**Millions of Americans rely on USPS for mail-in ballots, bills, paychecks, medication, and more._Your Post Office could be closed!_**
[**So we need a SURGE of support before it’s too late:
Do you APPROVE of your local Post Office?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=11&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI) [](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=12&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI) | [I APPROVE OF MY LOCAL POST OFFICE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/PostOfficePoll/?source=VPP_20260412_EMPOLL_E5_T0_AC_RS&t=13&akid=23643%2E878179%2ECKNvkI)
---

Your input is vital — please join 29 other local Democrats in responding by midnight!

_The Voter Protection Project_
878179
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

| | Dear No Tax Oregon Volunteer,Because of your incredible hard work, dedication, and commitment last fall, the Gas Tax Referendum officially qualified for the ballot as Measure 120. Thanks to you, the people of Oregon will have the opportunity to make their voices heard on those devastating tax increases.But we are now facing a new threat. And [**we need your help once again**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=43be9701-afa0-464c-9232-a9df568d24b5).Salem just reached back into your pocket to steal your tax cut. Last year, Congress made changes to the federal tax code that created a state tax cut for Oregonians–no tax on tips, no tax on overtime, no tax on social security, and more.

Politicians in Salem didn’t like this and decided to pass SB 1507, disconnecting portions of Oregon’s tax code from the federal tax code and creating a $300 million tax increase gut-punch to small businesses and working families.This week, Governor Kotek put the nail in the coffin by signing SB 1507 into law.**[But there’s still time to right this wrong–and we need your help.](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=70122731-ece3-4867-8658-b68dfc71d50e)** Mere hours after Governor Kotek signed SB 1507 into law, No Tax Oregon, Representative Ed Diehl, and Representative Dwayne Yunker filed a referendum to give voters a say on the clawback.We [**need volunteers**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=47e425d6-d884-46db-8edd-6f5a7311b11b) to help make the No Tax Clawback Referendum successful. No Tax Oregon has received criticism as being a one-time backlash to a poorly constructed tax plan. They are wrong. It’s time to show the critics that No Tax Oregon is a well organized movement with one purpose: **defending Oregon taxpayers from the robbery Salem has perpetrated on them for decades.** Your signature gathering, your conversations, and your dedication to defending your fellow Oregonians made Measure 120 possible. We are asking you to stay involved and gear up with us as we continue the fight.During the No Tax Clawback Referendum, we will be increasing the number of Petition Captains – those volunteers who helped us organize our ground game – and opening more signing locations across the state. We plan to reach more Oregonians than ever before and you can help us do that.Please sign up to volunteer by visiting [**NoTaxOR.com**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=26978e41-15db-40b4-b96f-7300c8379a43) and letting us know how you would like to help.
---

[**VOLUNTEER**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=c4a2b607-33a8-44e3-8ec5-0ad4dd44ac4b)
---

Together, we can continue the progress we’ve made in making Salem listen to the people of Oregon.**Round 2 starts now.** Thank you for your dedication, your time, and your belief that Oregonians deserve a voice. And thank you for joining us in the next chapter of Oregon history.Fighting for Oregon, Jodie Fleck
Grassroots Director
Oregon Freedom Coalition
P.S. Volunteering isn't the only way to fight back. Whether you're hitting the streets, writing a check, or both, every contribution brings us closer to success. You can donate at [**NoTaxOR.com**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=40c16e57-3e60-471a-9155-f47eb4a97b4d) alongside signing up to volunteer.

|

---
| **_JACKSON COUNTY_**** _WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW:_**
The petition drive is already underway! Petitioners began preparing hard-copy signature sheets the very next day after the bill was signed. The name of this petition is "No Tax Clawback"! The petition number is 2026-303. Petition deadline: June 4, 2026.
* Monday: Original signed petitions will be delivered to the Secretary of State.
* Tuesday: Copies will be made and FedEx’d to Team Captains across the state.
* Wednesday/Thursday: Packets should arrive at the Jackson County Republican Office (and other locations).

Vickie Westbrooks will be serving as the Team Captain for Jackson County, and we’re actively recruiting additional 4 teams of co-captains for each area of Jackson County. We want people from their cities. We need Medford, Phoenix-Talent, Ashland and Central Point. If you’re interested in helping lead this effort in your area, please let me know!
Important: Everyone who wants to collect signatures must complete a short training session. This ensures we follow all rules and maximize our success.
**How to Get Involved:**
Please sign up to volunteer by visiting [**NoTaxOR.com**](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=26978e41-15db-40b4-b96f-7300c8379a43) and letting us know how you would like to help.

**Mark Your Calendars!**
**SIGN ACROSS OREGON** Saturday, April 25th & Sunday, April 26th. This is our big push! We’re aiming to gather the approximately 80,000 signatures needed in approximately 20 days. We accomplished this in 10 days back in November 2025 — so we know it’s possible!
We need signature collectors and locations all across Jackson County. If you can secure a spot at a business, store, or public location (with owner permission, of course), we’d love your help. I have several promising locations and ideas in mind — just ask!
Pro Tip: Work in teams of at least two. It’s safer, more efficient, and gives you backup for breaks.
This is not only important — it’s genuinely fun! You’ll be amazed at how appreciative people are when they see you out there fighting for lower taxes and more freedom for Oregon families.
Let’s do this together! Please share this message widely and reach out if you can help.
Thank you,Vickie (Phillips) WestbrooksTeam Captain – Jackson CountyNo Tax Clawback Petitionevents@jcor.gop

---
---
| **2026 National Day of Prayer, Thursday, May 7 th****
****The National Day of Prayer has a long history****and became****public law on May 5, 1988.****The law****states:** **_The President shall set aside and proclaim the First Thursday in May in each year, as a National Day of Prayer, on which the people of the United States may turn to God in prayer and meditation at churches, in groups, and as individuals._********Find information from the National Day of Prayer Task Force at:** **[https://www.nationaldayofprayer.org/](https://app.dvcomms.net/api/Communication/Click?prm=MbIJ7QHiWuivoqqogirwlpBkeUQ4ZWfIn0GDIdO_iG3HxHcEFpM6GDPJGjKrEqhgifh5i5UxI2Za-zMEO27AYm3V41qc1w3wtnRgVul0o0EqKNoGS60FZTa1XdcNWJ59O1jhSMcEHqSeyCOJbp5hFYzrsaDPUwYoF7a_lFRXxT6FT8Cmw_q4O82IPUZbEKYuRUDDE_eGRL9mqILQKw0Y5mBBVfHbisuZDT_zytKcsZk1&target=05e1f144-c24e-4230-9a2b-87c70f0862e0)** **** **Sunday, May 17 th – Additional Day of Prayer****President Donald J. Trump revealed plans for a national gathering centered on prayer. As he explained:** **_“On May 17, 2026, we are inviting Americans from all across the country to come together on our National Mall to pray, to give thanks, and to rededicate America as one nation under God.”_********The event, called the National Jubilee of Prayer, Praise, and Thanksgiving, is envisioned as a day of worship, reflection, and communal prayer. Organizers anticipate participants from every state gathering in Washington to give thanks for 250 years of providential history and to pray for the years ahead.****
****In Jackson County, while Americans unite on the** **National Mall to pray, give thanks, and rededicate our nation as “one nation under God” some are planning to gather at the City Court House.**

clean_body: | | | | Poker - Candidates - Open House"No Tax Clawback" Referendum | | | | | Best of all, a generous portion of the Poker Tournament proceeds will benefit Recovery Cafe – Recovery Cafe-Reclaiming Lives, a cause close to our hearts. Call us today at 541-770-5277 to reserve your seat before they’re gone! We can’t wait to see your smiling faces around the table again for a night of fun, laughter, and friendly competition.** | | | | Brad Hicks is running for State Senate District 3 because Southern Oregon deserves a voice that will fight back against the failures coming out of Salem—a voice that says enough is enough. After decades of creating jobs and standing up for local businesses, Brad is ready to put common-sense principles back to work for our communities. | | | Commissioner Colleen Roberts is running for Re-Election! We are proud and fortunate to have Colleen as our Jackson County Commissioner and as our Jackson County Republican Party Vice-Chair!**** We wholeheartedly endorse Colleen and look forward to four more years of her leadership. You can find information about Colleen Roberts on her**** website: reelectcolleenroberts.com | Candidates for Governor**This year we are fortunate to have so many qualified Republican candidates running for Governor. Take a look at each of them and see what they have to say about the issues you care about.Look at their experience, their backgrounds and make note of their contact information. Get involved. Go to Meet and Greets for the candidates, go to forums and other opportunities to see what they have to say. If they've held office before, research their voting record and scroll through all their websites. Ask yourself: Is it in step with what you want to see for Oregon? We are “hiring” one of these hard working people to lead our state, we need someone with grit and determination. We need someone who will get Oregon back on a strong, positive course for us, our children and our grandchildren. And most importantly: VOTE. | | Danielle Bethell | | Chris Dudley | | Christine Drazan | | Ed Diehl | | Paul Romero | | David Medina | | | | | | | Dear No Tax Oregon Volunteer,Because of your incredible hard work, dedication, and commitment last fall, the Gas Tax Referendum officially qualified for the ballot as Measure 120. Thanks to you, the people of Oregon will have the opportunity to make their voices heard on those devastating tax increases.But we are now facing a new threat. And just reached back into your pocket to steal your tax cut. Last year, Congress made changes to the federal tax code that created a state tax cut for Oregonians–no tax on tips, no tax on overtime, no tax on social security, and more. Politicians in Salem didn’t like this and decided to pass SB 1507, disconnecting portions of Oregon’s tax code from the federal tax code and creating a $300 million tax increase gut-punch to small businesses and working families.This week, Governor Kotek put the nail in the coffin by signing SB 1507 into law. Mere hours after Governor Kotek signed SB 1507 into law, No Tax Oregon, Representative Ed Diehl, and Representative Dwayne Yunker filed a referendum to give voters a say on the clawback.We to help make the No Tax Clawback Referendum successful. No Tax Oregon has received criticism as being a one-time backlash to a poorly constructed tax plan. They are wrong. It’s time to show the critics that No Tax Oregon is a well organized movement with one purpose: defending Oregon taxpayers from the robbery Salem has perpetrated on them for decades. Your signature gathering, your conversations, and your dedication to defending your fellow Oregonians made Measure 120 possible. We are asking you to stay involved and gear up with us as we continue the fight.During the No Tax Clawback Referendum, we will be increasing the number of Petition Captains – those volunteers who helped us organize our ground game – and opening more signing locations across the state. We plan to reach more Oregonians than ever before and you can help us do that.Please sign up to volunteer by visiting and letting us know how you would like to help. Together, we can continue the progress we’ve made in making Salem listen to the people of Oregon.Round 2 starts now. Thank you for your dedication, your time, and your belief that Oregonians deserve a voice. And thank you for joining us in the next chapter of Oregon history.Fighting for Oregon, Jodie Fleck Grassroots Director Oregon Freedom Coalition P.S. Volunteering isn't the only way to fight back. Whether you're hitting the streets, writing a check, or both, every contribution brings us closer to success. name: The Flyover
subject: McIlroy Wins, Hungary for Change, and Whistling the Anthem
body:
Rory McIlroy won his [second straight Masters](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuu4yAYhVeDO6yfH2zHBcU07jLtlBbhYRhhiIAJ8u5Hie5dwa3PQ-c7f-77XYUY0vFbnVY2l_dke422NVv2U4dY8tVDqv5fOlS5XC7aq7Qj4ExBUMZ3o0K8djEY6axFywcr2bLMsIhlZYOXelphWYV-cMa4ERNDA3oSt49buccQ5LsNBOMMYOYwgpkQJ7dwgWDQIRFgTWi5jH9zSM1bF6_8smXU-Ryi9K09K-G_CG4Et977mB76TfHWCW71mUurBLcjR0dwO1VttlT6YSi5XPSLk_ocTaU9JFqtzsnQ2ooKh2_0O1N0UpzDersNRZpnDzGGOi9EwHGqED-L2o9ufEn8HwAA__9JL4h3) yesterday at Augusta National, becoming just the fourth golfer in tournament history to go back-to-back. He's the first since Tiger Woods in 2002.

| | |

# Monday, April 13, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1742, George Frideric Handel’s _Messiah_ premiered in Dublin, debuting a work that would become one of the most famous choral pieces ever written. [Take a listen here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1u6yAYxvGrwVusly_jMzAcqcpSpVKHtspkYfNiqDBEmAT57qv0Fro-j_Qb_l-X6WJCDGl9Mxvq6vKUsO0Ra8UybUuIJR8tpN3f02rK4XJZvEkTAzacQJwon6wJ8ZhEZ7VDZMg71FSpAZRQ_2jntZTO8mGkjqvRiNk5O4-LNGqEBSTlQxf0UwNBOQUYOPRgJWPSKS4YWOYYEYA21Fz67xxS9ejikR9Y-iVvXdS-1ttO-H_CzoSdW2v9ke_1PuPzfy6mLp7w84PwlzZv6-f761VeP7qi7a2FGMM-KCJg3UyIv2T9U4eHZj8BAAD__y18cTM). Nearly two-thirds of parents with Gen Z kids say their [adult children still rely on them](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFurDAQheGnMZ2RPfbC3cLFbeg2bUpkYIwnGmxkvIvI00ebV0h9pE9H_-djfHhiSuuH39DVkMeE58FYK5Zxm4lLvk5KR3ym1Zcr5DJHn0ZQ0EllpTbj4omv0TaLC4iApkGn-75Tve3vuonu7uFuOq28CdCBNsboWYOePHZ-gulfQ-6tKauNVqozqlXLDeAWemNBLRBAWIUL1Vzar0ypRgx85ReWds5bwy7Wuh_C_BcwCBjO82znNM3vUcDwpgUMygoYNAgYNp8uuWKS39IvT66HPCoxyxWrDJR8msmzjMi7DCVvskakIndfMNWjjXXjprhlP4mZjq4XVq2bJ_79Uv8U8OXgJwAA__8PpYQZ) financially, as we report in _Finance_. What's your take: is it the economy, or is this generation just not in a rush to leave the nest? Hit reply and let us know. If you’re a _Flyover Podcast_ listener who wrote in to say you missed Ayla Brown last week, be sure to [tune in today](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykz7Fu6yAUgOGnMVuswwFD7sBwl2zp2hGdwCGmwhBhmshvX7XqG3T_9Un_-9VfKZdc72-0sRup-cqvvfAY3P0WcunteOW6r5_1Tv1IrYeVqkdAcwJ9kspHyuXwWkSXmJGVYCetNWC1_SfF6miRRpGl801LY0OMROYcYQmLgaDOSmT3rYGWSgIYBTPEBXFJVmmEiAknDRzzaH3-aLmOlVM52pP7HNomilvHeOyT-j_hZcJL51unGudyTHj5DR8tdg5ch-guPl65lLwbO2m4b5TLDzP-9P50-BUAAP__Yk1uLw). She’s back after a lovely family vacation in the Caribbean and sounds refreshed. Mother’s Day is coming up fast, so we pulled together our [2026 Mother’s Day Gift Guide](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uKyEQxfGnWTpbs8OXXVDcxp1vmxIRMywTsWCx2Na-feQ0eYDU5-hX_D-u_hq4cF3-h5XcSM1Xem2FxqDu1xuX3vYX1y0_6hL6nlq_5VA9ApoDqMMsfQxcdq9EdIkISQpys7UGrLLnWWRnjD1pSJYgKpQS5acGlc7a6PNJ4ikJdm8N1CxnACPhCFEj6mSlQoiYcFJAkUfrx6_GdWRKZW9P6sdbW0VxeYz7Nsl_E14mvPzOC6exPDjS-zfhZW0jU98OMeyiu3h_cSm8GTspWNbA5Ycbf2rwdPgdAAD__x6qcEo) to help you find something she’ll actually love. ## Iran Talks Collapse, Trump Orders Blockade
Peace talks between the United States and Iran [collapsed on Sunday](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOtCAUhZ8GOwxeUMaC4m_s5m-3NAxcxruLMEEc4z79xn2F7U5yknPyfR_3-W4pUnr-tyuaGvKc8Ngi1oplXh3Fks-D0rbs6WnLGXJxi00zCBi4ULyTs7cUz1k13gREQNmg6bQehFZ67JrF9KO_2d5Kpx83DaN2VlmhFGi8QRBj35C51oTqZCfEIEUrfA_QBy0VCA8BmBLoqebSfmZKdcEQz_zG0rq8NtEstb42Jv8xmBhMx3G07rFdFFfPYLoig6mWfX3xrRZLlefAl1zW_Zs_YnZf1iOnYhODqSnGvw6KkbZBMyWeq6X4-1T_pOdt4CcAAP__3-96Hg) after more than 21 hours of negotiations in Islamabad, with Vice President JD Vance saying Iran refused to abandon its path to a nuclear weapon. President Trump responded by announcing a [naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuu2zAQRVcjdRSooT52wSKNOqdNKdDkSJyAH4EcW1FW_-C3hVdf4B6c8-exPgwFSvtvE1HzlteEZw3IjGWNlkLJ10mp-lfaTbm2XKw3aQUJk5CD6NXqDIVrHVqnN0RA1aLu53mS8zDf-9brm7TjeHMw3k0vb3aaxqdTT6sQJmXds29Jf97k0KteyknJTroRYNxmNYB0sEEzSHTEuXR_MyX2uIUrv7F0Nsc2aM981Eb9amBpYDnPs9vyv4_FZ29gOXIgJlsbWJgiBkoouLziUQVWa4JhSrtgX9BwFVRMEpWLIRY-l_j63xbtjpNCoDrNzSD3aCh8s_lHwd4avgIAAP__tj-Cjw), ordering the Navy to interdict any vessel that had paid a toll to Iran for passage through the critical waterway. U.S. Central Command has announced that it will begin a [blockade of all Iranian ports](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0M2K2zAUBeCnkXcK8rVikYUXUzqm0yGlKf2BbsyVfRWrI0tBuo5Jn764rzC7szgc-M6v83BGH3y8fsGFOnZpiLSVQMyUh2X0IafH5mOZ13jF_HApjzPGARS0UmlZN8OEPjwGXU2dIwJqKupqY1pltDnV1dyhVae6JWMtaIPGOTuiA-uaUwtobVP5bl9Tum5qpdpGHdR0BDg602hQEzgQWtHkOeXDn-Qjz-TCI90pH8a0VKGbmW9FNE8CegH9tm0HmwqnCMedspcE9HsU0Ic0YhDQc16Xm-Q5EzLFIgtn9CznlJf1r7QhjW84kUTHlOVapM8Y5UhYyPlMkjG8FUlxkpvnOa0s8ZqJFoosoNeX15cnfXn98fnDp-8vPzX8_vrx3H67PD8L6KvcTbfNh-BLa4RW1wV9-C_hd91_7-BfAAAA__-Qdpyg) on Monday at 10 a.m. ET. CENTCOM said the blockade would be “enforced impartially against vessels of all nations." But it will allow ships traveling between non-Iranian ports to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps warned that [military vessels approaching the strait](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAQgOGnwZ2jYcAmKSiucZdrr7SIGcKcMERAEnmffpV9ha1_6Sv-f9f16jhxvv91O9keyprp3RL1TnXdN061HG_OLT7z3dUjlLpFl1cEnEfQo1Srd5yOVQ_eBiIkNZCVxsxgtLnIIdqJpDorNYdZXpSZLnDznjaaCDe4SUUD248GWioJMCs4gZ8Qp2CURvAYUGggz73U0__CuUcK6Sgvqqet7EOysfdHE-qPwEXg0iNFTunTBC6Pkng7BC6cO9XsOpfsksBlOqMxoMdnG7m6PLZeHfcxlro_v8adMzWBy1Ctf7w5JW6zERruu-Mfeui_evWy-B0AAP__3BV97g) would be met with force, radioing a U.S. destroyer, _"This is the last warning."_
## Hungary's Orbán Ousted after 16 Years
Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán [conceded defeat on Sunday](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAUhJ_GdEbHP-DdwsVt6Pa2KZHBx-DE2KuDdxFvHxHlDdKNNJoZffPxGB8uppiX_25DW0MZMx57wlqRxm2Oicp5xLyvr7w4OkOheXV5lCB7DpoLNXoX0znqxtuAKFE1aIUxPRht7qJZrXK3_g7OmZufRNfdjQqTMF6FTmkAMTfRXm2ghRIAvYIWfCdlF4zSErwMkmlAH2uh9rPEXFcM6SxvpHYuW5PsWutzZ-ofkwOTw3Ec7TztF8XlMzlcksnhl4AvmJFc4phwrrFk_o5ftRAvNLnMn1iR-OaW0xEn3F-pXuGGrH8eMaW494ZpWDYX089-_dNpbyu_AwAA__8G8oLS) after 16 years in power, calling the result clear and painful as opposition leader Péter Magyar's Tisza party surged toward a supermajority in parliament. Magyar, a 45-year-old former Orbán ally who [broke with the ruling party](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGO5CAURE-DMyz4xrgdEGzirDfd0PptPoYVhhZmGvn2I88VJiuppCq99--5PjHEkPa_eJCpLq-J2hmpVirrsYVY8tVCOv1X2rFcLpfNY1pBgOZCcTmsFkO8VtVZ44iAho6MnCYtJjXNsvNGqYedpRhxluNLwWveEEdB5LRWmxx1F8y9JpQcpBB6EL2wI8DopkGBsOCAKUE21Fz6_zmk6snFK3-o9Fs-umh8re-TDX8YLAyW1lp_oc_5bhksNw6DBUsNW6Q7No_15C_yIVn-5pUKP3C_sJxcgtIgHwC9r0fsirHvFmIMp56YEvuBIf6c1l-Z-hj4DgAA__-RHXyI) two years ago, built a cross-ideological coalition fueled by voter frustration over economic stagnation, corruption, and crumbling public services. Turnout hit a record 79%, the highest in Hungary's post-Communist history, as thousands of Magyar supporters flooded the banks of the Danube in Budapest to celebrate. The result marks a shift for Hungary's foreign policy, as Orbán had cultivated [close ties with Russian President Vladimir Putin](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLESmzAQRL8GOjHiEMguVKRx57QpGSGdzDlCx0iyCX-fIb-QdndmZ9_79ZyfliKl10-7oamB54RHiVgr5nlzFDOfB6WyftLL5jNwdqtNM0iYhFSiH2ZvKZ6zar0JiIBDi6bXepJa6XvfriboZZymaUEJegS3qPu9V_bmhuBu0C9jS-Zak6ofeimnQXbSjwBj0IMC6SFAoyR6qpy7N1OqK4Z48hdz53hro1lr3Usz_Gjg0cDjOI4u8J-L4uqvhHP0DTy-9LtyFpwXm4Tj5NBjER4D2ip437lQJU4iovWYxYrWF7FzxVTJRlE-O-bNvjlTPcVBqc3G7wfFSGXSjZKvzVL896n-l8ivgb8BAAD__1yii5Y) and positioned himself as a key ally of President Trump in Europe.
## Rory McIlroy's Repeat Masters Victory
Rory McIlroy won his [second straight Masters](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDuu4yAYhVeDO6yfH2zHBcU07jLtlBbhYRhhiIAJ8u5Hie5dwa3PQ-c7f-77XYUY0vFbnVY2l_dke422NVv2U4dY8tVDqv5fOlS5XC7aq7Qj4ExBUMZ3o0K8djEY6axFywcr2bLMsIhlZYOXelphWYV-cMa4ERNDA3oSt49buccQ5LsNBOMMYOYwgpkQJ7dwgWDQIRFgTWi5jH9zSM1bF6_8smXU-Ryi9K09K-G_CG4Et977mB76TfHWCW71mUurBLcjR0dwO1VttlT6YSi5XPSLk_ocTaU9JFqtzsnQ2ooKh2_0O1N0UpzDersNRZpnDzGGOi9EwHGqED-L2o9ufEn8HwAA__9JL4h3) yesterday at Augusta National, becoming just the fourth golfer in tournament history to go back-to-back. He's the first since Tiger Woods in 2002. McIlroy held a [six-shot lead](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDFuxCAUBU-DOyz8jWFdUKSh27QpETEfQ4RhBWQt3z7aKDdI_aSnmfm4m7uNKeb93R6oui8m49kS9o7VHFtMtVxnzC18593Wy5e6BZsNMBCUcTrNxtmYLsMHpzwi4DygmqQUTHK5TkNQ_HOTsIITE1p5WzfJuQAml2X1blk5G6J6vTE-zRNjYmYjcwvA4uXMgTnwQDhDF3up41eJuQf06SpPrONWjiGp0PujkfmNgCagz_McsT3yaySg95I8Ad16qRcBbQjo6AhofuPLdJOCgK6lXvRPlSa0rlFBWyi90YadzoKGkpBW3Ep19LCtY21DVe5xxpRiE5Jwth82pl-g_q-KTwU_AQAA__9bqoUD) through the tournament's first two days, the largest in Masters history. On Saturday, however, he gave it all back and entered Sunday tied with Cameron Young for the lead. He regained control on the back nine yesterday, hitting a now-iconic shot on hole 12 to separate him from the pack. ([Watch it here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFu6yAUQL8Gb4kul2tjBoYnPXlLValDRwuZ65gKQwQkqf--Sn-h6xnOkc7nZb64EEO6vrmdbVvznPhZI7fGZd6XEEs-niHV7Z6urhxrLsvm0oyAwwnoJNXsXYjHTJ23KzOy6thKrQfQpI3sNquHZURwhg1JaVYcjfEelOqX0UuzyC7Ylw1IKgkwKDiD7xH7VStC8LiiIGAfWi7nrxxS23iNR35wOS9576LdWrtVof4JnARO3y8qcPq45dJqTu8l7Cxwqs21exU4IZAikv0IvQQiM2gUaqpC_UfoivW3Z4gx1EELguvuQvzNtD-9eVj8CQAA__8uN3QK).) He finished at 12-under, one stroke clear of Scottie Scheffler in second place. It's his sixth career major title. [McIlroy joins](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO2yAUhZ_G3rDwheBkYOji7e_a0bqBi6HFEMHNH_ntq0R9g65HR0fn-359bV-Ycir7TzzIcqhboVfPxExtO1zKrZ6vVHp8lh3bGWpzEcsGEoyQWsxq85jyuenR20AEpEay87IYuejlNo_RXiHcr8YHeSftwXt3BeMJ1VVrZZy7jcm-16Se1SylUXKS_gJwCYvSID0EGLQkn7i26XdNhSOFfNZvapOrx5htZH70Qf0YYB1g7Y_auE8nxlrfhQHWveYwwIqNk8s0wHpgZ2pdfChabaf4RyoCOuoips7vtDYRCRvfG-EfUYvoz-LxFMgCn_uzMwpQclZwu5gp8pHHZv3jlXJO3SyDlvuBKX9u8n-5_bbwNwAA__81h4_y) Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo, and Tiger Woods as the only repeat Masters champions.

One thing I've been talking about for a while now, even before Donald Trump got back into office, is that he has monetized the presidency.
## [Trump Didn't Drain the Swamp. He Monetized It.](https://substack.com/redirect/ec0f0f7c-af78-402a-82a3-2415c7e92476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [The Angry Democrat](https://substack.com/profile/102996536-the-angry-democrat)| | *
---|---|---
| Jan 21
[](https://substack.com/redirect/ec0f0f7c-af78-402a-82a3-2415c7e92476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Let's call it what it is -- corruption wrapped in a red hat.](https://substack.com/redirect/ec0f0f7c-af78-402a-82a3-2415c7e92476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/ec0f0f7c-af78-402a-82a3-2415c7e92476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
And I don't mean that in a vague or rhetorical way. I mean it directly.
Across pardons, foreign relationships, crypto, and real estate, you keep seeing the same pattern. Business activity ramps up around people close to him, capital moves, new ventures appear, and it all seems to align with political positioning. You have crypto projects forming before he is sworn in, token issuances tied to his name, from Trump coin to Melania coin, and the ability to generate massive amounts of capital off that attention. You have real estate conversations tied to geopolitical dynamics, and prediction markets where large, well-timed bets line up with major events.
And before getting into the example below around [World Liberty Financial](https://substack.com/redirect/8340cb85-5ca4-4060-b86e-04208ba96891?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), I think it is important to be clear about something most people either misunderstand or choose not to think about.
Corruption is rarely obvious.
It is not someone handing over a bag of cash in exchange for a favor. That version is easy to spot and easy to prosecute. Real corruption is more sophisticated than that.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a7b787eb-d9af-4a86-a730-3b5a6e32ea01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Cartoon Corruption 1883NThe United States Senator Of The Future - Is This What We Are Coming To American Cartoon Commenting On The Power Of The All-Mighty Dollar Over Members Of The Senate Cartoon 17
It is doing favors now for access later. It is building relationships that create leverage. It is shaping laws and regulatory environments that benefit your interests without ever having to say it outright. It is supporting someone politically and being rewarded with influence, contracts, or access. It is ignoring conflicts of interest instead of confronting them.
It is money moving at the same time decisions are being made. It is campaign donations followed by public funds being directed in certain ways. It is companies getting hired for major projects and then realizing the same networks of people are connected on both sides of the deal.
It is insider trading. It is financial engineering. It is smoke and mirrors.
People are looking for the obvious version of corruption, and when they do not see it, they assume nothing is there. But the reality is, the more sophisticated the system, the less obvious the corruption becomes. It is built through incentives, relationships, and timing.
So when you look at something like World Liberty Financial, or any of the ventures tied to Trump's orbit, the question is not where the smoking gun is. The question is who benefits, how they benefit, and what decisions are being made at the same time that money is moving.
This is the exact dynamic being described below in Peter Girnus' tweet.
* * *
_Note: This tweet is satirical commentary written in the first person of a fictional Web3 ambassador. The satire is built on verifiable public information. Written as a example of how corruption happens_
[| | | | | Peter Girnus 🦅
---
@gothburz
I am a Web3 Ambassador at World Liberty Financial. There are 12 of us on the team page. 4 are named Trump. 3 are named Witkoff. The page calls us "the passionate minds shaping the future of finance." 600,000 wallets bought our memecoin. They lost $3.87 billion. The family
| 7:07 PM * Apr 11, 2026 * 2.2M Views1.01K Replies * 4.31K Reposts * 13.1K Likes
---](https://substack.com/redirect/42fbdea4-3003-4aac-ade7-d4680e014232?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

I am a Web3 Ambassador at World Liberty Financial.

There are 12 of us on the team page. 4 are named Trump. 3 are named Witkoff. The page calls us "the passionate minds shaping the future of finance."

600,000 wallets bought our memecoin. They lost $3.87 billion. The family collected $350 million in trading fees. It launched 3 days before the inauguration. 80% of the supply went to CIC Digital LLC and Fight Fight Fight LLC. I did not choose the names. I designed the allocation, the vesting, the timing, and the distance between the product and the President.

The distance is my best work.

I am the reason these events are unrelated.

World Liberty Financial sends 75 cents of every dollar to DT Marks DEFI LLC. That is the family entity. Zero capital contributed. Zero liability assumed. I wrote this into the Gold Paper. Page 14. The lawyers bound it in white leather. The binding cost more than the due diligence.

Justin Sun invested $75 million. He was facing SEC fraud charges. The SEC dropped the case. He is now our advisor. These events are unrelated.

Changpeng Zhao pleaded guilty to federal money laundering violations. He received a presidential pardon. The SEC dropped its lawsuit against his exchange the same week we listed our stablecoin. Then the exchange settled a $2 billion deal entirely in that stablecoin. These events are unrelated.

Arthur Hayes, Benjamin Delo, and Samuel Reed of BitMEX pleaded guilty to Bank Secrecy Act violations. All 3 received presidential pardons. Then the company itself was pardoned. $100 million in fines. Gone. An American first. These events are unrelated.

Sheikh Tahnoun of Abu Dhabi paid $500 million for a 49% stake that was never publicly disclosed. Then the administration approved semiconductor exports to his companies over national security objections. These events are unrelated.

Everything is unrelated. I track the unrelatedness on a dashboard I built. The dashboard has 7 columns now. I am proud of the dashboard.

On May 22nd, 220 people paid a combined $148 million to eat dinner with the America First president. Over half were foreign nationals. Justin Sun paid $18.5 million for the first seat. He visited the Executive Office Building the day before. I designed the seating chart. I put it on the Investor Confidence page. That page is doing well.

The team page lists 3 Witkoffs. All 3 are Co-Founders.

Steven Witkoff is the President's Middle East envoy. He testified as a character witness at the President's fraud trial.

His son Zach runs the crypto operation. His son Alex is also a Co-Founder. I have not been told what Alex co-founded.

The father runs the diplomacy. The sons run the platform. The family runs both. That is organizational efficiency.

Barron is 19. His title is Web3 Ambassador. The same as mine. Donald Jr. called the conflicts of interest "complete nonsense." Eric launched a Bitcoin mining company called American Bitcoin. America First. The mining partner is Hut 8. Hut 8 was founded in Canada. America First means the name.

On March 6th, the President signed Executive Order 14233 creating a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. The order directs the government to hold Bitcoin. The President's family holds billions in Bitcoin. The executive order appreciates the President's assets by presidential decree. I did not write the executive order. I made sure it looked unrelated to the portfolio.

Trump Media put $2 billion of Bitcoin on its balance sheet. The ticker symbol is DJT. His initials. The press secretary said it is absurd to insinuate the President profits off the presidency. Forbes calculated his crypto holdings exceed the combined value of Mar-a-Lago and Trump Tower. I would call that absurd too. That is my job.

600,000 wallets bought in. 1 of them asked why she could not withdraw her funds. I told her the protocol was experiencing dynamic market conditions. She asked what that meant. I sent her the Gold Paper. She said she had read the Gold Paper. I muted her channel. Dynamic means the conditions change. The condition that changed was her access.

A congressman called us the world's most corrupt crypto startup operation. We put it on a coffee mug. Ironic merchandise. $45. The revenue split on the mug is also 75/25.

My own tokens vest on a different schedule. I wrote that schedule. That is not in the Gold Paper.

The memecoin funds the family. The family funds the platform. The platform funds the stablecoin. The stablecoin funds the deals. The deals require the pardons. The pardons free the partners. The partners fund the platform. The President signs the executive orders. The executive orders inflate the assets. The assets fund the family.

I am the reason these events are unrelated.

Until You Look Closer | | | Apr 13 | | | | | | | | | | | | One thing I've been talking about for a while now, even before Donald Trump got back into office, is that he has monetized the presidency. | | | | ---|---|--- | Jan 21 | And I don't mean that in a vague or rhetorical way. I mean it directly. Across pardons, foreign relationships, crypto, and real estate, you keep seeing the same pattern. Business activity ramps up around people close to him, capital moves, new ventures appear, and it all seems to align with political positioning. You have crypto projects forming before he is sworn in, token issuances tied to his name, from Trump coin to Melania coin, and the ability to generate massive amounts of capital off that attention. You have real estate conversations tied to geopolitical dynamics, and prediction markets where large, well-timed bets line up with major events. And before getting into the example below around , I think it is important to be clear about something most people either misunderstand or choose not to think about. Corruption is rarely obvious. It is not someone handing over a bag of cash in exchange for a favor. That version is easy to spot and easy to prosecute. Real corruption is more sophisticated than that. | | ---|---|--- Cartoon Corruption 1883NThe United States Senator Of The Future - Is This What We Are Coming To American Cartoon Commenting On The Power Of The All-Mighty Dollar Over Members Of The Senate Cartoon 17 It is doing favors now for access later. It is building relationships that create leverage. It is shaping laws and regulatory environments that benefit your interests without ever having to say it outright. It is supporting someone politically and being rewarded with influence, contracts, or access. It is ignoring conflicts of interest instead of confronting them. It is money moving at the same time decisions are being made. It is campaign donations followed by public funds being directed in certain ways. It is companies getting hired for major projects and then realizing the same networks of people are connected on both sides of the deal. It is insider trading. It is financial engineering. It is smoke and mirrors. People are looking for the obvious version of corruption, and when they do not see it, they assume nothing is there. But the reality is, the more sophisticated the system, the less obvious the corruption becomes. It is built through incentives, relationships, and timing. So when you look at something like World Liberty Financial, or any of the ventures tied to Trump's orbit, the question is not where the smoking gun is. The question is who benefits, how they benefit, and what decisions are being made at the same time that money is moving. This is the exact dynamic being described below in Peter Girnus' tweet. Note: This tweet is satirical commentary written in the first person of a fictional Web3 ambassador. The satire is built on verifiable public information. Written as a example of how corruption happens I am a Web3 Ambassador at World Liberty Financial. There are 12 of us on the team page. 4 are named Trump. 3 are named Witkoff. The lawyers bound it in white leather. The binding cost more than the due diligence. Justin Sun invested $75 million. He was facing SEC fraud charges. The SEC dropped the case. He is now our advisor. These events are unrelated. Changpeng Zhao pleaded guilty to federal money laundering violations. He received a presidential pardon. The SEC dropped its lawsuit against his exchange the same week we listed our stablecoin. Then the exchange settled a $2 billion deal entirely in that stablecoin. These events are unrelated. Gone. An American first. These events are unrelated. Sheikh Tahnoun of Abu Dhabi paid $500 million for a 49% stake that was never publicly disclosed. Then the administration approved semiconductor exports to his companies over national security objections. These events are unrelated. Everything is unrelated. I track the unrelatedness on a dashboard I built. The dashboard has 7 columns now. I am proud of the dashboard. On May 22nd, 220 people paid a combined $148 million to eat dinner with the America First president. Over half were foreign nationals. Justin Sun paid $18.5 million for the first seat. He visited the Executive Office Building the day before. I designed the seating chart. I put it on the Investor Confidence page. That page is doing well. The team page lists 3 Witkoffs. Barron is 19. His title is Web3 Ambassador. The same as mine. Donald Jr. called the conflicts of interest "complete nonsense." Eric launched a Bitcoin mining company called American Bitcoin. America First. The mining partner is Hut 8. Hut 8 was founded in Canada. America First means the name. On March 6th, the President signed Executive Order 14233 creating a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. The order directs the government to hold Bitcoin. The President's family holds billions in Bitcoin. The executive order appreciates the President's assets by presidential decree. The press secretary said it is absurd to insinuate the President profits off the presidency. Forbes calculated his crypto holdings exceed the combined value of Mar-a-Lago and Trump Tower. I would call that absurd too. That is my job. 600,000 wallets bought in. 1 of them asked why she could not withdraw her funds. I told her the protocol was experiencing dynamic market conditions.
